arts.ttl: Real Madrid boss Carlo Ancelotti reaches impressive La Liga milestone
arts.sum: Real Madrid travelled to Balaídos on Saturday evening to take on Celta Vigo in La Liga action. Regardless of the result, one person in the Real Madrid camp will be…

arts.ttl: CONFIRMED lineups: Celta Vigo vs Real Madrid, 2024 La Liga
arts.sum: Real Madrid have published their starting lineup for the upcoming match against Celta Vigo in the first LaLiga game since the FIFA break. Real Madrid starting XI: Courtois, Vazquez, Militao, Rudiger, Fran García, Tchouameni, Valverde, Camavinga, Bellingham, Vinicius, Mbappe.

arts.ttl: Vitor Roque levels Real Madrid pair Vinícius Jr. & Kylian Mbappé
arts.sum: A head-turning statistic, surrounding the goalscoring exploits of on-loan Barcelona striker Vitor Roque, has come to the fore in Spain on Saturday evening. This comes after the Brazilian found the…

arts.ttl: Details: The changing value of Real Madrid’s Nico Paz buyback clause
arts.sum: The details of La Liga giants Real Madrid’s buyback clause on youngster Nico Paz have this weekend been revealed. This comes on the same day that the 20-year-old has continued…

arts.ttl: Real Madrid possible starting XI against Celta Vigo today in LaLiga
arts.sum: As is typical in his pre-match press conferences, Ancelotti did not reveal his starting eleven for the Celta match but left one key decision up in the air: the inclusion of Valverde or Rodrygo . Who will start for Real Madrid against Celta?

arts.ttl: Serhou Guirassy: Europe's most in-form striker gunning for Real Madrid
arts.sum: In fact, since Guirassy returned to fitness, and including his blank against Heidenheim, no player in Europe's top-five leagues has scored more for club and country than his quite incredible 12 goals in nine appearances. He also won the penalty for Emre Can to make it 2-2 in that game, while his Champions League debut for Dortmund ended with a goal after Guirassy was introduced on the hour mark and with the score level at 0-0.

arts.ttl: Carlo Ancelotti confirms Real Madrid's transfer plans amid Trent Alexander-Arnold interest
arts.sum: Carlo Ancelotti has insisted he has not asked those in charge of Real Madrid to sign any new players in the January transfer window, despite speculation Los Blancos could launch an ambitious bid for Liverpool right-back Trent Alexander-Arnold. Ancelotti, however, believes the current Madrid squad is strong enough to cope without Carvajal and rejected suggestions of a January move for Alexander-Arnold.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid boss Ancelotti provides David Alaba injury update
arts.sum: Real Madrid head coach Carlo Ancelotti has this weekend moved to quell concerns surrounding the wellbeing of David Alaba. For those who may have missed it, reports began to surface…

arts.ttl: Arne Slot reveals Trent Alexander-Arnold's response to Real Madrid interest
arts.sum: Liverpool manager Arne Slot insists Trent Alexander-Arnold has not been distracted by reports of Real Madrid's interest in his services. Slot went on to confirm that he does have an input in Liverpool's contract decisions, but declined to reveal any of his suggestions to club officials.

arts.ttl: Liverpool receive Trent Alexander-Arnold boost as Carlo Ancelotti drops Real Madrid transfer hint
arts.sum: Real Madrid manager Carlo Ancelotti suggests that the Spanish champions will not make a move for Liverpool's Trent Alexander-Arnold in the January transfer window.
